{{< include /_macros.qmd >}}

# Scaling Laws {#sec-scaling-laws}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## In this chapter
<!-- The empirical regularities that let you predict loss from parameters,
     data, and compute — and use them to allocate a budget. -->
:::

**Learning objectives.**

<!-- TODO -->
- [ ]
- [ ]

## Power laws in N, D, and C

<!-- Loss as a power law in parameters, data, and compute
     [@kaplan2020scaling]. The functional form and how it's fit. -->

## Compute-optimal training

<!-- The Chinchilla trade-off between model size and tokens; how to split a
     fixed compute budget. Connects to the FLOP budget in @sec-the-transformer. -->

## Uses and limits

<!-- Predicting runs before launching; where the laws break; what they don't
     capture (downstream ability). -->

## Exercises {.unnumbered}

<!-- TODO: fit a toy scaling law from a sweep. -->

## Further reading {.unnumbered}
